data_in.description: preferred feature table (TSV from asari processing)
ionization mode (positive or negative) inferred from experiment metadata
optional adduct configuration files (JSON) for positive and negative modes

data_out.description: empCpd.json file containing empirical compound groups with fields: list_of_features, mz, rt, adduct annotations, and isotopologue assignments
experiment.json updated with empCpd moniker and metadata

domain_knowledge: Empirical compounds group isotopologues (e.g., m+13C3) and in-source adducts (e.g., [M+H]+, [M+Na]+) that represent the same chemical entity and co-elute chromatographically.
Default khipu tolerances are 5 ppm mass accuracy and 2 seconds retention time window; tighter tolerances reduce false-positive grouping of unrelated features.
Charge states up to z=3 and common solvent/chromatography-dependent adducts (e.g., [M+HCOO]− in negative ESI) must be pre-specified or inferred from ionization mode.
Singleton features (not clustered with any isotopologues or adducts) cannot receive Level 4 formula annotations because their adduct state is ambiguous.

expert_review: assess whether empirical compound groupings in ARTIFACT-EMPCPD-JSON are chemically sensible given the input mz/rt tolerances and feature table composition
evaluate whether the mz/rt tolerance parameters used are appropriate for the LC-MS platform and metabolomics study design

experiment.json updated with empCpd moniker and metadata
finding: The build_empCpds command groups features into empirical compounds by matching isotopes and adducts with configurable mz tolerance (default 5 ppm) and rt tolerance (default 2 seconds), producing a JSON file containing grouped features with their mz and rt values.
inputs: preferred feature table (TSV from asari processing)
ionization mode (positive or negative) inferred from experiment metadata
optional adduct configuration files (JSON) for positive and negative modes
methodology_summary: Load the preferred feature table with m/z, retention time, and intensity columns.
Apply pairwise feature distance calculations (m/z in ppm, rt in seconds) to identify candidates for grouping within user-specified tolerances.
Assign isotopologue and adduct labels based on theoretical mass differences and charge patterns, constrained by ionization mode and adduct list.
Cluster features into empirical compound groups, recording the list of constituent feature IDs and median m/z and rt per group.
Serialize groups to JSON with all required fields (list_of_features, mz, rt, adduct, isotope annotations) and validate structure.
Validation: verify empCpd.json is well-formed JSON, contains ≥1 empirical compound group, and each group has non-empty list_of_features, numeric mz, and numeric rt fields.

title: Reconstruct the EmpCpd construction step and verify the empCpd.json output structure
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: experiment.json updated with empCpd moniker and metadata.
workflow_description: 1. Load the preferred feature table (TSV format) from the asari_results subdirectory. 2. Invoke khipu via the build_empCpds command with configurable m/z tolerance (default 5 ppm) and retention time tolerance (default 2 seconds), specifying ionization mode and adduct definitions for the detected polarity. 3. Construct feature groups by clustering features within the specified m/z and rt windows, assigning isotopologue and adduct annotations based on observed mass differences and charge state patterns. 4. Serialize the empirical compound collection to JSON format, including list_of_features, median m/z, median rt, and pre-annotation fields for each group. 5. Validate JSON structure and confirm all required fields are present.
